title: AI Avatar Video Creator for Fast Growing Wellness Brand (Ongoing Ad Production)
description:
We are is a fast growing DTC beauty and wellness brand. Our hero product, Collagen Jelly Sticks, is in the middle of a major paid media push across Meta and TikTok, and we're scaling our creative output with AI generated avatar videos.



What You'll Do



You'll produce AI avatar videos that look and sound like high quality UGC ads. We'll provide briefs, scripts, and reference angles. You'll bring them to life using AI avatar platforms, voice generation tools, and finishing techniques that make the output feel native to social feeds rather than obviously AI.



Typical work includes:



Generating AI avatar videos using tools like HeyGen, Arcads, Captions AI, Hedra, Argil, Creatify, or Synthesia

Selecting or cloning voices (ElevenLabs or similar) that match the avatar and audience

Adapting our scripts into multiple hook and CTA variants for ad testing

Adding B roll, product shots, captions, music, and sound design to finish each video

Quality control on lip sync, pacing, and natural delivery so the ads don't feel robotic

Delivering in 9:16, 1:1, and occasionally 16:9, typically 15 to 60 seconds



Who You Are



Hands on experience producing AI avatar videos at volume, ideally for paid social ads

Strong opinions on which avatar platforms perform best for which use cases (and why)

Skilled with voice cloning and AI voice tools (ElevenLabs, PlayHT, or similar)

Comfortable in a finishing tool (CapCut, Premiere, DaVinci, or Final Cut) for B roll, captions, and polish

You understand direct response ad structure: hook, problem, solution, proof, CTA

Sharp eye for catching the small tells that make AI video look fake (and how to fix them)

Reliable turnaround (typically 48 to 72 hours per batch)

Comfortable working async via Slack, 
, or Google Drive



Bonus points:



Experience in beauty, wellness, or supplement DTC

Ability to write or rewrite hook variations from a base script

Familiarity with Meta and TikTok ad creative best practices



What We Offer



Ongoing work with consistent weekly volume (5 to 10 videos per week to start, with room to scale)

Clear briefs, written scripts, product footage, and direct founder feedback

Long term partnership for the right person, with first look at additional brand projects



To Apply

Please include:



Two or three examples of AI avatar videos you've produced (ideally for paid ads)

Your rate (per video or hourly) and typical turnaround

The word "jelly" at the top of your application so we know you read this carefully





Looking forward to seeing your work.

title: Assistant Property Manager
description:
Assistant Property Manager

Aoede LLC | 100% Remote | Full-Time



About the Role



The Assistant Property Manager is responsible for the day-to-day operations, support, and customer service of a growing, diversified real estate portfolio across Texas and California. This includes a 70-unit commercial building in Houston, TX, single-family rental properties in California, and short-term rental (Airbnb) properties in Austin, TX. This is a 100% remote, full-time, long-term position.



We do annual team bonding trips in the Philippines — if you're talented, hardworking, and ambitious, we'd love to meet you in person and talk about where your career is headed.



What You'll Do



- Direct and oversee daily operations across a mixed portfolio of commercial, single-family, and short-term rental properties

- Provide excellent customer service to all tenants, guests, and prospects

- Prepare and send communication notices to tenants

- Draft lease agreements and manage property documentation

- Coordinate maintenance requests and vendor communication across multiple property types

- Enter accounts receivable and accounts payable into QuickBooks

- Perform monthly bank reconciliations

- Ensure accuracy and timely preparation of all required reports

- Schedule tea
etings, coordinate via Zoom, and delegate tasks to tea
mbers

- Manage Airbnb listings, guest communications, and short-term rental operations



What We're Looking For



- 2+ years of property management experience

- Experience supporting US-based properties preferred

- Experience with any combination of commercial, residential, or short-term rental management is a plus

- Proficiency in QuickBooks

- Expert-level use of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)

- Familiarity with property management software (AppFolio a plus)

- Comfortable using Asana, Slack, and Zoom for daily communication

- Strong written and spoken English

- Detail-oriented, organized, and able to manage multiple priorities across different property types

- Bachelor's degree preferred

- Humble, coachable, and eager to learn US property management regulations (Section 8, rent control, tenant/landlord laws in California and Texas)



What We Offer



- 100% remote position — work from home

- Long-term role with real growth potential

- On-the-spot bonuses for high performers

- Raises considered at performance evaluations for those who master the training material and execute at a high level

- Extensive runbooks and tutorials to support your development

- Annual team bonding trips in the Philippines

- A team culture that values hard work, initiative, and continuous learning



---



Our Hiring Process



1. Apply — Submit your application with the details listed below

2. Initial Review — We review your experience and qualifications

3. Assessment — Qualified candidates receive a TestGorilla skills assessment (~1 hour, webcam required)

4. Interview — Candidates who score at or above the 50th percentile are invited to a Zoom interview

5. Offer & Onboarding — Successful candidates begin with a 3-month probation period with full training and support



---



Frequently Asked Questions



What types of properties will I manage?

You'll support a diversified portfolio that includes a 70-unit commercial building in Houston, single-family rental homes in California, and short-term rental (Airbnb) properties in Austin. This role gives you broad exposure across multiple property types.



What are the working hours?

You will be expected to work US Central Time business hours. This is a full-time position (40 hours/week).



How and when do I get paid?

Pay is on the 1st and 15th of each month via Wise (
).



Is this freelance or full-time?

This is a full-time, dedicated position. Freelancing or side work is not allowed. We need your full commitment.



Do you provide equipment?

You must have your own laptop, headset, and reliable high-speed internet connection.



Is there a probation period?

Yes, there is a 3-month probation period. After that, performance evaluations are conducted every 6 months.



How do raises and bonuses work?

We offer on-the-spot bonuses for exceptional work. Raises are considered at your 6-month performance evaluations based on how well you learn the training material and execute your responsibilities.



What training and support do you provide?

We have extensive runbooks, tutorials, and experienced tea
mbers to help you get up to speed. We invest in people who are willing to learn.



What software will I need to learn?

AppFolio (property management), QuickBooks (accounting), Asana (task management), Slack (communication), and Zoom (meetings). If you don't know all of these yet, that's okay — what matters is your willingness to learn quickly.



---



To Apply



Please include:

1. A brief summary of your property management experience

2. Which Western countries you've supported (if any)

3. What types of properties you've managed (commercial, residential, short-term rental)

4. What PM and accounting software you've used

5. Your availability to start



About Aoede LLC



Aoede LLC is a Texas-based property management company overseeing a growing, diversified portfolio of commercial, single-family, and short-term rental properties across Texas and California. Founded with the mindset of a startup but built on enterprise-level professional experience, we move fast, build smart, and hold ourselves to a high standard.



Our team of 10+ professionals spans multiple countries and operates fully remote, managing everything from a 70-unit commercial building in Houston to Airbnb properties in Austin and single-family rentals in California. We hire talent from around the world — including the Philippines, Vietnam, India, Sri Lanka, Pakistan, Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, South Africa, Venezuela, and beyond. We don't care where you sit. We care how you perform.



What makes us different: we combine the speed and scrappiness of a startup with the processes, structure, and professionalism you'd expect from a much larger organization. We invest heavily in documentation — our runbooks and tutorials are built so that the right person can get up to speed quickly and operate independently. We reward performance, not tenure. If you learn the material, execute at a high level, and take ownership of your work, you'll grow here.



Our culture is built on communication, collaboration, and humility. We operate as a team — not in silos. Everyone, from leadership down, is open to feedback and willing to hear what they need to do to improve. We're not perfect and we don't pretend to be. What we do is strive for continuous improvement and learning every single day. Leadership is genuinely invested in finding better ways to do things, and we expect the same mindset from everyone on the team.



A Note on Honesty



We value honesty over impressive claims. Please don't tell us you're an expert in something you've only read about or briefly touched. It's okay to say "I've used AppFolio a few times but I'm not an expert" or "I haven't handled evictions before but I'm willing to learn." We will find out the truth — whether it's during the assessment, the interview, or the first week on the job. And when we do, it damages trust that's very hard to rebuild.



We would much rather hire someone who says "I don't know that yet, but I'll learn it" than someone who claims to know everything and can't deliver. Being humble, honest about where you are, and willing to put in the work to grow — that's what gets you far here. We have the training, the runbooks, and the team support to get the right person up to speed. What we can't teach is integrity.



We're actively expanding our portfolio and our team. We do annual team bonding trips because we believe the people behind the work matter just as much as the work itself.



This is a company where your work matters, your voice is heard, and your growth is directly tied to what you put in.

title: Direct Response Video Editor (Meta Ads)
description:
Performance Video Editor (Meta Ads) – ELYVIA Skincare

Are you a Meta Ads expert who can blend cinematic B-roll storytelling, traditional UGC, and AI-assisted content into high-converting skincare creatives?



We are building ELYVIA, a premium UK-based skincare brand focused on innovative at-home infusion treatments. We don’t just sell products; we sell a new biological belief through education and high-impact direct-response marketing.



We are looking for a Creative Editor who understands that a winning ad is a mix of tight psychology, visual retention, and the ability to "show, not just tell."



ABOUT THE ROLE

This is not a basic "cut-and-paste" role. You will be responsible for turning detailed briefings into polished Meta ads. We need an editor who is a master of versatility:



Master the Scroll-Stop: Craft hooks that immediately stop our target audience (Women 40+) in their tracks using high-quality B-roll or UGC.



Versatile Content Creation: You must be able to build powerful ads using a mix of traditional UGC, cinematic B-roll, and AI-generated avatars/UGC when the script requires it.



Visualize the Mechanism: Use B-roll and animations to explain how ELYVIA  works.



Maintain ELYVIA Branding: Every ad must feel premium and credible. We want u to be able to make branded ads.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

Direct Response Editing: Create high-impact 9:16 video ads (VSL-style) focused entirely on performance and conversion.



Strategic Sourcing: We provide a base set of assets (B-roll & VO), but we expect you to source your own additional visuals to perfectly match the narrative and fill any gaps. 



All-Round Footage Handling: Whether it’s raw customer UGC, professional product shots, or AI-generated footage, you can weave it into a seamless story. 



Mechanism Animations: Source or create clean visuals that show our products, the more branded the better.



THE WORKFLOW & PAID TEST

We believe in clear direction but highly value own creativity. For every project, you will receive:



A Written Script (the narrative flow).



A Visual Briefing (Istructions on which visuals you can bind to the script).



A Base set of B-roll and assets.



Paid Test:

We start with a paid trial video. Since our scripts are comprehensive and focused on deep education, the test video will be over 60 seconds.



Fixed Test Fee: $10



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

Proven experience editing Meta Ads, VSLs, or performance-driven UGC.



Expertise in sourcing high-quality B-roll that fits specific psychological triggers.



Proficiency in software like Premiere Pro, After Effects, DaVinci, or CapCut Pro.



Experience with AI video tools / AI UGC.



Ability to work independently and bring your own creative "flair" to a briefing.



We are specifically looking for someone who understands:



how to make the first 3 seconds scroll-stopping

how to keep visual pacing tight

how to make scripts easy to follow visually

how to source relevant B-roll

how to create visual contrast between “problem” and “solution”

how to edit before/after proof sections

how to use animations for mechanism explanation

how to make skincare ads look premium and credible

how to edit for Meta ads, not YouTube or long-form content



COMPENSATION & GROWTH

We are looking for a long-term partner, not a one-off freelancer.



Ongoing: Consistent weely workload as we scale.



Growth: Potential to grow into a Lead Editor role with performance-based bonuses for "Winning Creatives."



HOW TO APPLY

Send your Portfolio.



Which editing software do you use?



Ready to help us define the future of premium skincare? Apply now.



Kind regards,



David

title: Senior Assistant Property Manager
description:
Senior Assistant Property Manager

Aoede LLC | 100% Remote | Full-Time



About the Role



The Senior Assistant Property Manager is a leadership role responsible for overseeing the day-to-day operations of a growing, diversified real estate portfolio across Texas and California. This includes a 70-unit commercial building in Houston, TX, single-family rental properties in California, and short-term rental (Airbnb) properties in Austin, TX.



You will lead and supervise a team of Assistant Property Managers while personally handling complex operational, financial, and tenant matters. This is not an entry-level position — we need someone who has managed US properties, can make decisions independently, and is ready to own outcomes.



This is also not a role where you simply delegate and disappear. You need to be hands-on, involved in ongoing tasks, and ready to step in with guidance when a tea
mber needs it — or take over a task entirely when the situation calls for it. Our leadership philosophy is trust but verify: you empower your team to execute, then you follow up and confirm the work was done correctly.



This is a 100% remote, full-time, long-term position.



We do annual team bonding trips in the Philippines — if you're talented, hardworking, and ambitious, we'd love to meet you in person and talk about where your career is headed.



What You'll Do



Team Leadership

- Supervise and mentor Assistant Property Managers using a trust-but-verify approach — delegate clearly, follow up consistently, and confirm quality

- Stay hands-on with ongoing tasks — review work in progress, not just final outputs

- Step in to provide guidance, coaching, or direct support when tea
mbers are stuck or struggling

- Take over tasks when the situation requires it — no task is beneath you

- Apply project management principles to plan, track, and deliver property operations initiatives on time and within scope

- Schedule tea
etings, delegate tasks, and hold tea
mbers accountable for deadlines and quality

- Conduct training sessions using company runbooks and tutorials

- Serve as the first point of escalation for the team before matters reach ownership



Property Operations

- Oversee daily operations across a mixed portfolio of commercial, single-family, and short-term rental properties

- Ensure excellent customer service to all tenants, guests, and prospects

- Manage Airbnb listings, guest communications, and short-term rental operations

- Coordinate maintenance requests and vendor communication across multiple property types

- Ensure accuracy and timely preparation of all required reports



Tenant Relations & Legal

- Handle escalated tenant situations — complaints, disputes, and difficult conversations

- Prepare and serve legal notices (pay or quit, lease violations, cure or quit)

- Coordinate eviction processes with legal counsel and local authorities

- Ensure compliance with US property management regulations including Section 8, rent control, and tenant/landlord laws in California and Texas



Financial Management

- Participate in budget planning for properties across the portfolio

- Negotiate vendor contracts and pricing to optimize operational costs

- Set and review lease pricing based on market conditions

- Enter accounts receivable and accounts payable into QuickBooks

- Perform monthly bank reconciliations

- Monitor rent collections, track arrears, and escalate delinquencies



Documentation & Communication

- Draft lease agreements and manage property documentation

- Prepare and send communication notices to tenants

- Coordinate via Zoom, Slack, and Asana daily



What We're Looking For



- 2+ years of US property management experience (required — non-negotiable)

- Proven experience managing commercial, residential, or short-term rental properties in the US

- Understanding of Project Management (PMP) principles — you should be comfortable with scope management, task prioritization, risk identification, stakeholder communication, and delivering results on schedule. PMP certification is a plus but not required — what matters is that you think and operate like a project manager

- Experience leading and managing tea
mbers — you must have real examples of how you've delegated work, verified completion, coached tea
mbers through challenges, and stepped in to take over when needed

- A hands-on leadership style — you don't just assign tasks and walk away, you stay involved and ensure quality at every step

- Experience handling tenant escalations, legal notices, and eviction coordination

- Understanding of US property management regulations — Section 8, rent control, fair housing, and state-level tenant/landlord laws in California and Texas

- Proficiency in QuickBooks

- Expert-level use of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)

- Familiarity with property management software (AppFolio strongly preferred)

- Comfortable using Asana, Slack, and Zoom for daily communication

- Strong written and spoken English — you will be communicating directly with US tenants, vendors, and legal contacts

- Financially literate — comfortable with budgets, vendor negotiations, and lease pricing decisions

- Bachelor's degree preferred

- Dependable, proactive, and able to make sound decisions without constant oversight

- Must provide 3 work references from US-based individuals who can verify your US property management experience (this is required and will be checked before any offer is made)



What We Offer



- 100% remote position — work from home

- Long-term role with real growth potential into senior management

- On-the-spot bonuses for high performers

- Raises considered at performance evaluations for those who master the training material and execute at a high level

- Extensive runbooks and tutorials to support your development

- Annual team bonding trips in the Philippines

- A team culture that values hard work, initiative, and continuous learning



---



Our Hiring Process



1. Apply — Submit your application with the details listed below

2. Initial Review — We review your experience and qualifications

3. Assessment — Qualified candidates receive a TestGorilla skills assessment (~1 hour, webcam required)

4. Interview — Candidates who score at or above the 50th percentile are invited to a Zoom interview

5. Reference Check — We contact your 3 US-based references to verify your property management experience

6. Offer & Onboarding — Successful candidates begin with a 3-month probation period with full training and support



---



Frequently Asked Questions



What types of properties will I manage?

You'll oversee a diversified portfolio that includes a 70-unit commercial building in Houston, single-family rental homes in California, and short-term rental (Airbnb) properties in Austin. You'll also lead the team responsible for day-to-day management of these properties.



How is this different from the Assistant Property Manager role?

The Senior APM leads and supervises the APM team, handles escalated tenant situations and legal processes, and is involved in financial decisions including budgeting, vendor negotiations, and lease pricing. You are expected to operate independently and make sound decisions.



What leadership style are you looking for?

We believe in trust but verify. You delegate tasks to your team, trust them to execute, and then follow up to confirm the work was done correctly. But you're not just a manager — you're hands-on. You stay involved in ongoing tasks, provide guidance when tea
mbers need it, and take over tasks yourself when the situation requires it. If you're the type of leader who only gives orders from a distance, this role isn't for you.



Do I need a PMP certification?

PMP certification is a plus but not required. What we care about is that you understand and apply project management principles in your daily work — scoping tasks clearly, prioritizing effectively, identifying risks before they become problems, communicating proactively with stakeholders, and delivering results on time. If you naturally think this way, you'll thrive here.



What are the working hours?

You will be expected to work US Central Time business hours. This is a full-time position (40 hours/week).



How and when do I get paid?

Pay is on the 1st and 15th of each month via Wise (
).



Is this freelance or full-time?

This is a full-time, dedicated position. Freelancing or side work is not allowed. We need your full commitment.



Do you provide equipment?

You must have your own laptop, headset, and reliable high-speed internet connection.



Is there a probation period?

Yes, there is a 3-month probation period. After that, performance evaluations are conducted every 6 months.



How do raises and bonuses work?

We offer on-the-spot bonuses for exceptional work. Raises are considered at your 6-month performance evaluations based on how well you learn the training material, lead your team, and execute your responsibilities.



What training and support do you provide?

We have extensive runbooks, tutorials, and experienced tea
mbers to help you get up to speed on our specific processes. While we expect you to bring US property management knowledge, we'll train you on our systems and workflows.



What software will I need to know?

AppFolio (property management), QuickBooks (accounting), Asana (task management), Slack (communication), and Zoom (meetings). Proficiency in AppFolio and QuickBooks is strongly preferred for this role.



Why do you require US-based references?

We require 3 work references from US-based individuals to verify your US property management experience. This is non-negotiable. References should be former supervisors, clients, or colleagues who can speak to your property management work in the US. We will contact all references before extending an offer.



---



To Apply



Please include:

1. A summary of your US property management experience

2. What types of US properties you've managed (commercial, residential, short-term rental)

3. Your experience handling tenant escalations, legal notices, or evictions

4. Describe your leadership style — give a specific example of how you've delegated a task to a tea
mber, verified the work, and stepped in when guidance was needed

5. Any project management training, certifications, or experience (PMP, Agile, etc.)

6. What PM and accounting software you've used

7. 3 work references from US-based individuals (name, company, role, phone/email)

8. Your availability to start



About Aoede LLC



Aoede LLC is a Texas-based property management company overseeing a growing, diversified portfolio of commercial, single-family, and short-term rental properties across Texas and California. Founded with the mindset of a startup but built on enterprise-level professional experience, we move fast, build smart, and hold ourselves to a high standard.



Our team of 10+ professionals spans multiple countries and operates fully remote, managing everything from a 70-unit commercial building in Houston to Airbnb properties in Austin and single-family rentals in California. We hire talent from around the world — including the Philippines, Vietnam, India, Sri Lanka, Pakistan, Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, South Africa, Venezuela, and beyond. We don't care where you sit. We care how you perform.



What makes us different: we combine the speed and scrappiness of a startup with the processes, structure, and professionalism you'd expect from a much larger organization. We invest heavily in documentation — our runbooks and tutorials are built so that the right person can get up to speed quickly and operate independently. We reward performance, not tenure. If you learn the material, execute at a high level, and take ownership of your work, you'll grow here.



Our culture is built on communication, collaboration, and humility. We operate as a team — not in silos. Everyone, from leadership down, is open to feedback and willing to hear what they need to do to improve. We're not perfect and we don't pretend to be. What we do is strive for continuous improvement and learning every single day. Leadership is genuinely invested in finding better ways to do things, and we expect the same mindset from everyone on the team.



A Note on Honesty



We value honesty over impressive claims. Please don't tell us you're an expert in something you've only read about or briefly touched. It's okay to say "I've used AppFolio a few times but I'm not an expert" or "I haven't handled evictions before but I'm willing to learn." We will find out the truth — whether it's during the assessment, the interview, or the first week on the job. And when we do, it damages trust that's very hard to rebuild.



We would much rather hire someone who says "I don't know that yet, but I'll learn it" than someone who claims to know everything and can't deliver. Being humble, honest about where you are, and willing to put in the work to grow — that's what gets you far here. We have the training, the runbooks, and the team support to get the right person up to speed. What we can't teach is integrity.



We're actively expanding our portfolio and our team. We do annual team bonding trips because we believe the people behind the work matter just as much as the work itself.



This is a company where your work matters, your voice is heard, and your growth is directly tied to what you put in.

mber to take ownership of three core marketing pillars.

 campaigns, and keeping our

 Community Responsibilities

Initiate conversations, polls, and mini-events to keep engagement consistent

title: Virtual Assistant for Film Research and Asset Sourcing for Instagram Reels
description:
I’m looking for a detail-oriented virtual assistant to help source visual assets for a recurring series of short Instagram/TikTok reels about international filmmakers, cinema history, and film culture.



For each video, you will receive a short script about a filmmaker. Your job will be to gather relevant images, article screenshots, and YouTube video clips that match the script and can be used by an editor.



This is a research-heavy role, so I’m looking for someone who is organized, fast, accurate, and interested in film, media, history, or online content.



For each reel/script, you will source:



-5 high-resolution headshots/images of the filmmaker

These should be large, clear, and usable in a video edit. No tiny, blurry, low-quality images.



-3 relevant vertical screenshots from articles.

These should visually support the script. For example, news articles, interviews, reviews, festival pages, historical sources, or articles about the filmmaker’s work.



-5 to 8 relevant video clips from YouTube

You will provide YouTube links, timestamps, and a short note explaining why each clip is useful. These could include interviews, behind-the-scenes clips, film excerpts, archival footage, trailers, or public talks.



Organize everything clearly

-You will place all links, notes, and files into a Google Drive folder and/or tracking spreadsheet so the editor can easily use them.



You are a good fit if you:



Have strong internet research skills

Can find high-quality images and useful video clips quickly

Can follow a script and understand what visuals would support each sentence

Are organized and reliable

Have good English reading comprehension

Are interested in film, filmmakers, documentaries, history, art, or culture

Know how to use Google Drive, Google Sheets, and YouTube

Can avoid irrelevant, low-quality, or incorrect assets



Experience with editing, content creation, film research, or social media is a plus, but not required.



PAY:



Pay is $5 USD per video, with each video expected to take around one hour.



We will start with a paid test assignment. If the test goes well, the first batch will likely be 20 videos, with potential for ongoing work after that.



How to Apply



Please reply with:



A short paragraph about your research experience

Any experience you have with film, YouTube, social media, editing, or content research

Your internet speed and availability

A sample of a filmmaker, artist, or public figure you’ve researched before, if applicable

Put the phrase FILM RESEARCH VA at the top of your application so I know you read the full post



Looking for someone detail-oriented, reliable, and excited to help build a high-quality film education series.

title: Calling All Client Onboarding Specialists!!
description:
WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING WITH US…



Ascend Innovation is a fast-moving, systems-driven company focused on helping businesses improve customer engagement through automation, AI tools, and streamlined processes.



We build structured systems that eliminate missed opportunities and help our clients operate more efficiently.



You’ll be joining a startup environment where systems matter, details matter, and your work directly contributes to real outcomes for clients.



We provide clear SOPs, structured onboarding, and ongoing support to help you succeed in your role.



We believe in long-term tea
mbers and actively look for ways to help you grow, take on more responsibility, and develop your skills over time.



You won’t just be completing tasks, you’ll be playing a key role in helping new clients join our company and building the systems that power our process.



THE ROLE WE ARE HIRING FOR



We are hiring a Client Onboarding Specialist (GoHighLevel)



This role is focused on onboarding new clients into our systems using GoHighLevel and following detailed SOPs to ensure every client account is set up correctly and efficiently.



Your daily and weekly tasks will include:



Creating and setting up client sub-accounts in GoHighLevel



Following step-by-step SOPs for client onboarding and system setup



Ensuring all client information is entered accurately and completely



Troubleshooting setup issues and identifying errors



Updating and improving SOPs as systems evolve



Managing and tracking onboarding tasks in Asana



Working with data and tracking progress in Google Sheets



Communicating clearly with the team regarding progress, blockers, and updates



This role requires someone who is highly detail-oriented, organized, and able to follow structured processes while also thinking critically when something doesn’t work as expected.



THE IMPORTANCE OF THIS ROLE



This is a critical systems role within our company.

You will directly impact:

The success of new client onboarding

The accuracy and reliability of our systems

The efficiency of our internal operations

The overall client experience

The right candidate will help ensure every client is onboarded smoothly while also improving the systems we use as we grow.



WHY WE’RE A GREAT PLACE TO WORK



We are building modern systems that combine automation, AI, and strong operational processes.

We move quickly, but we rely on structure and clear systems to stay organized and efficient.



We value tea
mbers who take ownership of their work, pay attention to detail, and care about doing things the right way.



You’ll be supported with clear documentation, defined processes, and a team that communicates openly and works collaboratively.



THE VALUES WE STAND BY



Systems create consistency

Details matter

We take ownership of our work

We improve and optimize continuously

We support each other as a team

We focus on real outcomes, not just activity



PAY AND HOURS



Starting rate:

 ?300–?400 PHP per hour (based on experience)



This position is available:

Immediately



This is a:

Long-term position



Provisional Schedule:

Monday – Friday

08:00 to 17:00 EDT (Florida, USA)



You will be available for:

30–40 hours per week



We offer:

Weekly pay

Performance-based bonuses

Opportunities for increased responsibility and compensation over time



NOW THE IMPORTANT PART – HOW YOU CAN APPLY:

If this role sounds like a great fit for you, we’d love to hear from you.



Please complete this short application form to get started:







We review applications based on form responses only.



You do not need to send a CV or resume at this stage.

We will contact shortlisted candidates after reviewing submissions.



Looking forward to reviewing your form application



Orion / Ascend Innovation

title: GUEST EXPERIENCE SPECIALIST — EVENING / LATE-NIGHT SHIFT
description:
Haus Vacation Rentals | 100% Remote | 32 hours/week





ABOUT HAUS



Haus is a boutique short-term rental management company running 14 properties across the Hudson Valley, Catskills, and Berkshires. We're a small team that takes pride in guests feeling like they're talking to a knowledgeable local, not a script. We pair professional systems with the personal touch of a thoughtful host.





THE ROLE



We need someone to own the evening and late-night guest experience — the after-dinner booking inquiries, the "we just got in and the lockbox isn't working" texts, the questions that come in while our daytime team is asleep. You'll be the person guests reach when most managers have gone dark.



This is a calm, focused shift. Most evenings are quiet with steady, manageable volume — but when something goes wrong at midnight on a Friday, you're the one who keeps it from becoming a 1-star review.





SCHEDULE



32 hours/week across 6 days. Tuesdays off.



In Eastern Time (where our guests live):

- Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday: 10:00pm–2:00am

- Saturday & Sunday: 6:00pm–2:00am



In Manila Time:

- Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday: 10:00am–2:00pm

- Sunday & Monday: 6:00am–2:00pm



If you live in another time zone, we can map it for you — but you must be working during US East Coast evening hours.





WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO



- Respond to guest messages on Airbnb, VRBO, 
, direct, and SMS — usually within 5 minutes

- Handle check-in issues: lockbox codes, WiFi, finding the property in the dark, "the heat won't turn on"

- Triage real emergencies (no water, power outage, broken lock) — coordinate with on-call vendors and escalate to the founder when warranted

- Log issues in Airtable so the daytime team picks up where you left off

- Reply to incoming booking inquiries and pre-stay questions — your responses convert lookers into bookings

- Monitor and respond to reviews when they post during your shift

- Keep clean handoff notes for the morning shift





TOOLS WE USE



Hostaway (PMS), Airtable (ops database), Slack (team comms), Quo / OpenPhone (calls and SMS), Google Workspace. Training provided — we care more about judgment and writing than tool experience.





WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR



- Writes like a person, not a script. "Hey — checkout's at 11, but I can stretch it to noon if the cleaner schedule allows. Let me check." Not "I would be more than happy to assist you with your inquiry regarding checkout."

- Calm under pressure. A guest texting in panic at 1am needs you to slow them down, not match their energy.

- Reads context. A short question gets a short answer. A worried guest gets a longer one.

- Reliable internet, backup power, quiet workspace, and a working headset. You'll be on calls.

- English fluency — both written and spoken.

- Hospitality, customer service, or STR experience is a plus but not required. Voice and judgment matter more.





COMPENSATION



$5.00 USD / hour, paid weekly via Wise.





HOW TO APPLY



Send the following to 



1. A short writing sample — pick one and write the response you'd send:



   - A guest texts at 11:47pm: "we're at the property and the lockbox code isn't working. we've been driving for 5 hours."



   - A guest sends an inquiry at 2am: "Is your cabin pet-friendly? We have a 60lb rescue dog who's well-behaved. Looking at the long weekend in October."



2. A 60-second voice recording — introduce yourself and tell us about a time you turned around a frustrated customer or guest. We want to hear your voice and how you tell a story.



3. A brief note on your current setup (internet speed, backup power, headset).



Attach your résumé too — but we'll read the writing sample and voice memo first.

title: Senior Procurement & Sourcing Specialist (Halal Food Supply Chain)
description:
About the Role



We are a growing halal food distribution and import/export company specializing in frozen poultry, beef, and essential food commodities. We are seeking a highly driven and experienced Procurement & Sourcing Specialist to lead supplier discovery, pricing negotiations, and supply chain development across the United States.



This is not an entry-level research role. We are looking for someone who can source, negotiate, and secure deals with real suppliers and contribute directly to revenue growth.



Key Responsibilities

-Source halal-certified suppliers for:

-Frozen poultry (drumsticks, leg quarters, wings)

-Beef cuts (ribs, rump, blade, etc.)

-Frozen vegetables (corn, fries, mixed veg)

-Canned goods (fish, fruits, vegetables)

-Identify and vet U.S.-based manufacturers, processors, and exporters

-Initiate and manage supplier communication 
, calls, follow-ups)

-Negotiate pricing, MOQ, and terms (CIF, FOB, etc.)

-Build and maintain a reliable supplier pipeline

title: YouTube Shorts Video Editor — Medical Podcast (HuMED)
description:
Hi! I'm Dr. David Spiro, host of HuMED — a podcast where I interview physicians about their stories, expertise, and the behind-the-scenes of modern medicine. Our YouTube channel has 60,000 subscribers and is growing fast. I'm looking for a reliable, creative video editor to help me turn our long-form podcast episodes into engaging YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels.

What you'll do:



Edit 8–12 vertical short-form videos per week (roughly 30–60 seconds each) from full podcast episodes I provide

Identify the strongest "hook" 
nts in each episode and cut them into self-contained clips

Add clean, accurate captions/subtitles (large, readable, properly placed in the safe zone)

Add light B-roll, zooms, or visual emphasis where helpful (no over-editing — keep it clean and professional)

Apply consistent branding (HuMED logo placement, colors, font — I'll provide a brand kit)

Export in 9:16 ratio, optimized for YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels

Deliver finished videos in a shared folder (Google Drive or Dropbox) on an agreed schedule



What I'm looking for:



Strong English comprehension — you need to understand spoken medical conversations well enough to identify the most compelling 
nts

Experience editing short-form vertical content (CapCut, Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, or similar)

A good eye for pacing, hooks, and what makes a clip "stop the scroll"

Reliable communication — responsive on
 during agreed hours

Self-starter who can work from a brief without heavy hand-holding

Bonus: experience with medical, health, education, or interview-format content



What I offer:



Steady, long-term work (this is not a one-off project)

Clear briefs, brand guidelines, and feedback so you can do your best work

Opportunity to grow with the show — more hours and higher pay as we scale

Respectful, professional communication



To apply, please send:



A short note about yourself and why you're interested

2–3 examples of vertical short-form videos you've edited (especially talking-head or interview-style)

Your hourly rate and weekly availability

Your typical turnaround time per Short



The first week will be a paid trial — I'll pay you to edit 3 sample Shorts so we can see if we're a good fit before committing long-term.

Thanks for reading — I'm excited to find the right person to grow this show with!

title: AI Growth & Automation Specialist (Healthcare – PCP & Pain Management)
description:
Role Overview (PLEASE read the whole description, the correct person will have endless opportunity, once they can prove themselves)



We are seeking a highly results-driven AI specialist to design, deploy, and optimize AI-powered lead generation and patient acquisition systems for a medical practice focused on Primary Care and Chronic Pain Management.

This role is not theoretical. The primary goal is simple:

Increase qualified patient volume and booked appointments using AI systems.

 

Core Responsibilities

1. AI Lead Generation & Patient Acquisition

• Build and deploy AI-driven funnels to generate high-intent patient leads 

• Optimize local search (Google, maps, SEO) specifically for: 

o “Primary Care near me” 

o “Pain management near me” 

• Implement AI-enhanced ad campaigns (Google Ads, Meta, etc.) 

• Create automated follow-up systems (SMS,
, voice) 

 

2. AI Agent Development (CORE FOCUS)

• Build AI agents that: 

o Respond to inbound patient inquiries instantly (web, SMS, phone) 

o Pre-qualify patients (insurance, symptoms, urgency) 

o Book appointments directly into the scheduling system 

• Develop conversational AI that mimics a front desk coordinator 

• Integrate AI agents with: 

o EHR / scheduling tools 

o Phone systems / call tracking 

• Ensure HIPAA-conscious workflows (no careless data handling) 

 

3. Automation & Conversion Optimization

• Design full patient journey automation: 

o Lead ? Response ? Qualification ? Booking ? Reminder ? Show-up 

• Reduce no-shows using AI reminders and re-engagement 

• Improve conversion rates from inquiry to booked visit 

 

4. Analytics & Performance Tracking

• Track and report: 

o Cost per lead 

o Cost per booked patient 

o Show rate 

o ROI on campaigns 

• Continuously optimize based on real data (not assumptions) 

 

Required Qualifications

• Proven experience in AI agent development (chatbots, voice agents, automation systems) 

• Experience in lead generation for local service businesses (healthcare preferred) 

• Strong understanding of: 

o CRM systems 

o Scheduling integrations 

o SMS/email automation tools 

• Ability to build end-to-end systems, not just pieces 

 

MANDATORY: Proof of Work (Non-Negotiable)

Applicants MUST provide:

1. Live Demo or Case Study Showing:

• AI agent handling real inbound leads (chat, SMS, or voice) 

• Automated booking flow in action 

• Real business use case (preferably healthcare or local service) 

2. Measurable Results

• Before/after metrics: 

o Leads generated 

o Conversion rates 

o Cost per acquisition 

• Screenshots or dashboard access preferred 

3. System Walkthrough

• Explanation of: 

o Tools used 

o Architecture of the AI system 

o How it integrates with scheduling/CRM 

 

What success looks like

Month 1: Fully operational lead-to-booking funnel live for both service lines

Month 2: 40+ booked new patient consults per week, tracked and reportable

Month 3: Hitting or approaching 100 consults/week target with a sustainable, low-touch system

Ongoing: Weekly reporting dashboard, continuous optimization, no-show reduction



Compensation

• Competitive base (or project-based) 

• Performance bonuses tied to booked patients 

• Opportunity for long-term engagement based on results



Please TEXT your name and information to

title: Full-Time Creative Virtual Assistant
description:
Full-Time Creative Virtual Assistant

Base Image Creation | High-Level Creative Team | Growth Opportunity



We are looking for a full-time Creative Virtual Assistant who can create high-quality base images made specifically for video advertisements.



This is not a video editing role.



Your responsibility is to create the foundation image — the main visual that everything starts from.



These base images will later be used by our animation team for B-roll creation and by our video editing team for final advertisements.



You are the first and most important step in the creative process.



If the base image is weak, unrealistic, or low quality, the final ad will fail.



You will work from storyboard briefs created by our video editors. Based on these briefs, you must create strong, realistic, high-converting visual assets that feel premium, trustworthy, and natural.



The standard is simple:



there should be almost no visible difference between your work and reality.



We are looking for someone with very high standards, strong taste, attention to detail, and someone who understands what makes an ad look expensive, trustworthy, and built to convert.



You will be joining a fast-growing international e-commerce brand with a 15-person marketing team focused heavily on performance marketing and creative testing.



You will be working inside an international team, so fluent English communication is required — both written and spoken.



What You Will Do



Create high-quality base images for advertisement use



Turn storyboard briefs into strong visual foundations for video ads



Build animation-ready assets that can later be turned into B-roll



Make sure all visuals are realistic, premium, and conversion-focused



Avoid unnatural visuals, obvious mistakes, and scam-looking creatives



Continuously improve outputs so ads feel real and trustworthy



Stay updated with the newest creative systems and workflows



Work closely with our creative, animation, and video teams



Understand that your work is the starting point of the entire ad production process



What We Need



Full-time availability



Around 50 hours per week



Fluent English communication (written and spoken)



Comfortable working inside an international remote team



Very high standards for realism and quality



Strong understanding of premium e-commerce creatives



Ability to identify when content looks fake or unnatural



Someone who works fast without sacrificing quality



A-player mindset and strong ownership



Someone who wants to grow with the team long-term



Someone who understands that details d
 performance



Salary



$700 USD per month



Growth Opportunity



This role is for people who want to stay ahead of the market.



You will work inside a high-level marketing team and continuously learn the newest systems, workflows, and ways to create better-performing creatives.



The goal is not just making images.



The goal is becoming someone who understands how winning creatives are built from the very first frame.



If you are serious, hungry, and want to grow inside a real performance-driven team, apply here:

title: B2B Appointment Setter (Cold Caller) – U.S. Dental Practices
description:
B2B Appointment Setter (Cold Caller) – U.S. Dental Practices



We are hiring a B2B appointment setter to call U.S. dental practices.



Todd Dental Operations helps dental offices recover revenue from missed appointments, inactive patients, unscheduled treatment, recall gaps, and front-office follow-up issues.



As TDO integrates to artificial intelligence outreach, candidate will eventually oversee outreach processes. 



This is a controlled outbound calling role focused on quality, professionalism, and accurate qualification — not mass dialing.



IMPORTANT (READ BEFORE APPLYING)



This role requires clear, professional English that U.S. business owners can easily understand on the first call.



You must be comfortable speaking with dentists, owners, and office managers.



This is not a multitasking job — you must be focused during calling hours.



If you are working multiple jobs, managing clients, or running your own business and cannot give full attention, do not apply.



If you do not have real cold calling experience, do not apply.



If you do not submit a voice recording, your application will not be reviewed.



COMMITMENT REQUIREMENT



This role requires focused attention during calling hours.



We are not looking for someone juggling multiple clients or treating this as a side task.



We are looking for someone who treats this as a primary role, can commit fully during scheduled calling hours, and takes ownership of results and performance.



This is a performance-driven position. Consistency and focus are required.



OPPORTUNITY



This is not a short-term gig.



Todd Dental Operations is a family-owned business under the Todd Family Trust, structured for long-term stability and generational ownership.



We are building a long-term operation and are looking for someone reliable who wants stability and growth.



If you perform and help generate real revenue, you will grow with the company.



We value reliability, discipline, professional communication, and clean CRM work.



COMPENSATION



Starting rate: $6–$10/hour (based on experience)

Paid trial: 10–20 hours



Pay increases:



Raise after 10-day trial

Raise after 3-week probation period



Performance bonuses:



$300 for each client that signs a contract

$100/month recurring for each active client you originated

No cap on bonuses



BONUS CONDITIONS



Bonuses are paid only when the client is a real, qualified dental practice, the appointment is properly documented in GoHighLevel (GHL), the call is recorded and verifiable, the client signs and remains active, and you remain in good standing.



Weak, fake, or unqualified appointments will not be counted.



EARNING EXAMPLE



5 clients = $1,500 signing + $500/month recurring

10 clients = $3,000 signing + $1,000/month recurring

20 clients = $6,000 signing + $2,000/month recurring



Strong performers can build long-term income.



RESPONSIBILITIES



Call assigned U.S. dental offices

Ask for dentist, owner, office manager, or decision-maker

Followscript (provided)

Log every call outcome in GHL

Write structured notes after meaningful conversations

Book only qualified discovery calls



REQUIREMENTS



Real cold calling experience (B2B preferred)

Clear, professional English (very important)

Confidence speaking with U.S. business owners

Strong attention to detail in CRM and notes

Ability to follow instructions exactly

Reliable internet and quiet environment



TO APPLY (REQUIRED)



1. Send a 60-second voice recording introducing yourself

2. Read this script clearly:



"Hi, this is calling on behalf of Todd Dental Operations. We help dental offices recover revenue from missed appointments, inactive patients, and unscheduled treatment follow-up. I was hoping to speak with the owner, dentist, or office manager."



3. Briefly describe your cold calling experience



4. Answer the following:

   Are you currently working other jobs?

   If yes, how many hours per day are you available for this role?



Applications without a voice recording will not be reviewed.



FINAL NOTES



This role will be actively monitored.

Call quality, notes, and professionalism matter more than volume.

This is a performance-based opportunity, not a passive job.



ABOUT US



Todd Dental Operations is a U.S.-based company founded by a retired United States Army Officer.



We operate with a highly structured system using established tools and workflows inside GoHighLevel (GHL). This is a disciplined, process-driven environment.



We are a family-owned business under the Todd Family Trust, built for long-term stability and generational ownership.



We focus on execution, accountability, and measurable results for dental practices.



We are building a performance-driven team and are looking for long-term tea
mbers who want to grow with the company.

title: Senior Residential Appraisal Report Writer
description:
I run a residential appraisal firm located in the Bay Area, California, and am looking for an experienced residential appraisal writer to join the team. The team includes 3 Bay Area appraisers and 8 employees in the Philippines. We are rapidly growing and are looking for an experienced and senior level residential report writer to join our team.



Your role will be to do the following:

1. Complete the entire residential appraisal report after the appraiser has provided you with the inspection information and comparables. This includes all the commentary, adjustments, and all other parts of the report. We will provide training on how we specifically expect the commentary and adjustments based on our own personal preferences, however you are expected to already have this type of experience working for other appraisers.

2. Review the work of other report writers on our team, provide feedback, and help train newer report writers on our team. You are expected to act as the "last line of defense" of the report before it is sent to the appraiser for their final review before submission. As such, we expect you to be able to review the report, find any issues, correct these issues, and then send to the appraiser a near perfect report.



To be considered for this role, you must meet the following criteria:

1. You have worked for a US residential appraiser

2. You have experience performing data entry for residential properties in the US

3. You have experience creating the 1004MC, selecting comparables, drawing the sketch, labeling photos, making adjustments to the sales grid, and making commentary for the addendum.

4. Ideally, you have experience managing other appraisal report writers.



If you don't have experience in writing commentary or the addendum, or do not have experience with making adjustments on the sales grid, we would still like to hear from you as we have a different job that would fit your experience better.



We are flexible with your work hours and offer Philippine daytime shift hours for your convenience. We would like to start part time but then transition to full time, so ideally you are open to working full time exclusively with us if its a mutual fit.



To be considered for this position, you must do the following in your application message (failure to do all of the following will result in your application being ignored):

1. How many years have you worked for a US residential appraiser? What tasks did you perform for them?

2. How many hours does it take you to complete one report? What parts of the report do you complete?

3. Do you have experience writing commentary for the addendum, drawing the sketch, labeling photos, and creating the 1004MC? Please indicate which ones you have experience with, and how many years for each.

4. Please provide a sample of at least one appraisal report you have fully completed. You can redact any private information from the report. Please let me know what parts exactly you completed on this report(s).

5. Provide a publicly accessible link to your resume.

6. Are you open to starting part time and then transitioning to full time? Once working full time with us, will you be working exclusively with us without any other jobs? When is the earliest you can begin working with us?

7. What are your preferred working hours?

8. Do you have experience managing other residential appraisal report writers? If so, please discuss in detail your job responsibilities.



Thank you, and I look forward to the opportunity to work together.

title: Google Ads & YouTube Ads Specialist (Expert Level) — Part-Time Contract
description:
Google Ads & YouTube Ads Specialist (Expert Level) — Part-Time Contract



We're a Mac-based software company in the live streaming and video production space. Our customers are creators, podcasters, streamers, and professionals who produce video content. We have an active and engaged community, and our product sells on a subscription model with a free trial entry point.



We're looking for a contractor who has been in the trenches with Google Ads and YouTube Ads — not someone who took a course last year, but someone who has managed real budgets, made real mistakes, learned from them, and has the results to prove it.



What you'll be working on:

------------------------------------

- Managing and optimizing existing Google Search campaigns focused on trial sign-ups and event/campaign registrations

- Running YouTube in-stream and discovery ads targeting creators and professionals

- Audience strategy — layering intent, interest, and remarketing properly

- Conversion tracking — you need to know what's firing, what's not, and why it matters

- Weekly reporting with actual insight, not just screenshots of metrics

- Testing ad copy, creative angles, and landing page recommendations based on what the data is telling you



You must have:

---------------------

- Proven experience managing Google Ads and YouTube Ads for a software or SaaS product

- Demonstrated ability to drive trial sign-ups or subscription conversions (not just traffic or clicks)

- Deep understanding of Google's campaign types, bidding strategies, and audience tools

- YouTube-specific knowledge — in-stream, bumper ads, video action campaigns, audience targeting

- Clean conversion tracking setup experience (Google Tag Manager, GA4, or equivalent)

- The ability to write ad copy and create graphics as needed— you should be expert level here

- References or case studies you can share. We will ask.

- Fluent in English, written and oral



What this is not:

----------------------

- A full-time role. This is a part-time contract, up to 15 hours per week.

- A training ground. We have an existing account. We need someone who can get in, audit what's there, and start improving it — fast.

- A set-it-and-forget-it job. We expect active management and real communication.



Logistics:

-----------------

- Remote contract position

- Up to 15 hours per week

- You'll report directly and communicate in English — written and verbal clarity matters

- Payment in USD



To apply:

---------------

Send your application with the following:



- A summary of your Google Ads and YouTube Ads experience, specifically with software or SaaS products

- Examples of campaigns you've driven - visuals, copy

- Results you've driven — be specific. Numbers, not adjectives.

- At least one reference we can contact, ideally from a client whose campaigns you managed

- Your hourly rate

- Read everything carefully before you apply. At the very end of your application, include a fun fact about Canada that you learned — and put that same fun fact in your subject line. Applications without it will not be reviewed, no exceptions.

title: AI & Workflow Automation Specialist — 2-Month Project (Australian Business)
description:
We are an Australian business looking for an experienced AI and workflow automation specialist for a 2-month project, with the possibility of extension based on performance.



This is a hands-on build role — not a planning or strategy role. You will spend the majority of your time designing and implementing automations that eliminate manual work across our business operations. By the end of the project, we expect to have a set of live, working automations that our team uses every day.



WHAT YOU WILL WORK ON:



• Product sourcing & supplier outreach — build automated workflows to contact factories in China and other markets, collect pricing, compare product offers, and track responses without manual follow-up

• Freight forwarding — automate the process of reaching out to freight providers, collecting quotes, and comparing costs side by side

• Warehouse & 3PL operations — conduct a full workflow analysis of our third-party logistics operation and identify and implement automations to reduce cost and handling time

• Invoicing & stock management — map our current invoicing and stock holding processes, identify inefficiencies, and build automations that improve cash flow and reduce excess stock

• General workflow automation — any other manual, repetitive process across the business that can be eliminated or reduced through AI and automation tools



WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU:



• At least 2-3 years of experience building real automations for real businesses — not just theory

• Hands-on experience with Make (Integromat), Zapier, n8n, or similar platforms

• Experience using AI tools including ChatGPT or Claude to automate content, communications, and data processing

• Ability to connect and integrate different business platforms via API or native connectors

• Strong written English — you will be communicating directly with our Australian team

• Self-directed — you can work from a brief without needing constant supervision

• Experience with procurement, inventory, logistics, or supply chain workflows is a strong advantage



HOW TO APPLY:



Do not send a generic application. To be considered, your application must include:



• A short description (3-5 sentences) of a specific automation you have built — what the problem was, what you built, and what it saved

• A list of the automation tools and platforms you use regularly

• Your available start date

title: YouTube Thumbnail Artist
description:
==============================

YouTube Thumbnail Artist Application

==============================



We’re looking for a visual storyteller who knows how to stop the scroll.

The "Living in Charlotte" team is growing, and we need a designer who understands that a thumbnail isn't just a "pretty picture"—it’s the front door to our business. We’re looking for someone who obsesses over click-through rates (CTR), understands human psychology, and can turn a real estate topic into a "must-click" visual.



If you’re the type of person who spends your free time analyzing why certain creators dominate the homepage, we want to talk to you.





IMPORTANT: Please read this entire listing. We’re big on the details here. The final step of our process involves a portfolio review and a reference check from a former client or team lead.



==============

ABOUT THE TEAM

===============





We operate like a fast-paced startup. We’re high-energy, high-standard, and we care deeply about the people we serve. If you have a growth mindset and love seeing your work directly impact a channel's success, you’ll fit right in.



What’s it like here? Check out this video to meet the crew. 



=============

Our Core Values:

=============



Delight the Client: We go beyond "good enough" to create visual 
nts that stick.



Master Your Craft: You aren't just using templates. You’re staying ahead of design trends and YouTube's ever-changing algorithm.



Follow Through, Always: Deadlines matter. When a video is ready to go live, the thumbnail needs to be ready to perform.





==========

ABOUT YOU

==========





Do you look at a boring Thumbnail and immediately see three different ways to make it visual? Do you understand how to use color, contrast, and facial expressions to create curiosity? Are you the person who catches a weird shadow or a messy crop that everyone else missed?

We need a high-level creative who wants to own the "packaging" of our brand. You should be comfortable taking raw ideas and turning them into high-performing assets without needing someone to hold your hand through the process.



====================

WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

====================





Design & Strategy



The "Hook": Create 3 - 7 main thumbnails with different variations. High-performing thumbnails per week that drive clicks.



Psychology-Based Design: Use depth, lighting, and "the gap" (creating a question only the video can answer) to increase CTR.



A/B Testing: Design variations to see which colors or layouts the audience prefers.

Photo Editing: Clean up property photos, color-grade portraits, and make every asset look premium.







Operations & Growth



Performance Tracking: Look at the data. If a thumbnail isn't clicking, you’re the first to suggest a pivot.

Asset Management: Keep our library of headshots and Charlotte-specific b-roll organized and ready to use.

Collaboration: Jump into brainstorming sessions to help us title videos before we film them so the visuals and words work together perfectly.





======================

WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

======================



A Killer Portfolio: You’ve done this before. You have examples of thumbnails that actually worked.



The Tools: You’re a pro in Photoshop or similar high-end design software. You also know how to use AI (like Midjourney or Generative Fill) to speed up your workflow, not replace your creativity.



Speed & Communication: We move fast. You need to be able to handle revisions quickly and keep us updated on Slack.



Charlotte Vibes: You don't have to live here, but you need to be able to capture the "vibe" of our city through your designs.



============

HOW TO APPLY

============





STEP 1: The Application Submit your info 
. 



To show us you’re paying attention to the details, please include the phrase "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.



STEP 2: Quick Chat If your portfolio blows us away, we’ll hop on a 15-minute intro call.



STEP 3: The Design Challenge We’ll give you a title and some raw assets and ask you to show us your magic. This is a paid trial task.



STEP 4: Reference Check We’ll chat with one person you’ve worked with in the past.



STEP 5: 90-Day Trial If we’re a match, we’ll start with a 90-day trial period. We’ll give you the training and support you need to master our systems. If you crush it, you’ll be a permanent part of the team.





Application form here

title: TradFi Trader Copywriter [$7-$10+/hr w/ Bonuses]
description:
Overview:



We’re building a trading-focused product where clarity of thought and narrative is the edge.

Most trading content is:

Generic

Lagging

Written by people who don’t actually understand markets



We do the opposite.

We’re looking for a TradFi-focused copywriter who thinks like a trader, not just someone who rewrites headlines.



What You’ll Do:

title: GoHighLevel Expert / Systems & Automation VA
description:
**Job Title:** GoHighLevel Expert / Systems & Automation VA



**About Us**

Bradley Floorings of Augusta is a flooring company based in Augusta, Georgia. We value **Unwavering Integrity, Unmatched Dependability, Community-Focused service, Customer Enjoyment, and Family-Oriented Values**. We are building stronger systems and looking for a reliable VA who can help us do it the right way.



**About the Role**

We are looking for a **highly dependable GoHighLevel expert** who can build, manage, troubleshoot, and improve our CRM, workflows, automations, and lead handling systems.



This is not just a task-following role. We want someone who can **think independently, communicate clearly, solve problems, document systems, and help us create clean, reliable processes**.



**Main Responsibilities**



* Build and improve GoHighLevel workflows and automations

* Manage CRM, pipeline stages, and lead flow

* Troubleshoot broken logic, duplicate triggers, and missed leads

* Test forms, automations, missed call text-back, and follow-up systems

* Document workflows and create simple SOPs

* Give clear daily updates and communicate proactively



**Bonus Skills**



* Building websites, landing pages, or funnels

* WordPress experience/ Site Ground

* Google Sheets/reporting experience



**What We Need**



* Strong GoHighLevel experience

* Clear written English

* Strong communication and follow-through

* Fast response time during agreed work hours

* Honest, proactive communication if something is delayed or broken

* Ability to test work carefully and keep systems organized



**Important**

Communication and dependability matter a lot to us. If something cannot be completed on time, we expect to be told **beforehand**, not after the fact.



**To Apply, Please Include**



1. A short introduction

2. Your Go High Level experience

3. Examples of workflows/automations you’ve built

4. Any website or landing page experience

5. Your typical response time during work hours

6. The word **DEPENDABLE** at the top of your application



We are looking for someone who wants to build something solid with us and take pride in doing excellent work. Start out part time, however, full time available depending on candidates progress.

title: Video Editor for Performance Ads Meta
description:
ABOUT US

We are a German Direct-to-Consumer supplement brand in the women's 

health space. Our entire creative workflow is AI-powered — from image 

generation to voice-overs to video clips. We produce high-performing 

direct-response ads for Meta and TikTok, targeting German-speaking 

women aged 35-55. (Brand name shared with shortlisted candidates.)



THE OPPORTUNITY

We are starting this role as PART TIME (around 20-30 hours per week). 

If you perform well during the first 4-8 weeks, we will upgrade you to 

FULL TIME with a higher monthly retainer. We are looking for a 

long-term editor, not a one-off freelancer. Our top performer can 

become our lead video editor and grow with the brand.



WHAT YOU WILL EDIT

60 to 180 second talking-head ads in UGC and authority style. Each ad 

combines:

- AI-generated video clips (Kling 3.0, Veo 3.1)

- AI-generated still images (NanoBanana Pro) that you will animate

- ElevenLabs voice-overs (in German — you do NOT need to speak German)

- Medical 3D visualizations

- Occasional real stock footage

All edited together in CapCut Pro.



REQUIRED HARD SKILLS

- Advanced CapCut Pro: keyframe animations, slide-in/pop-up overlays, 

  multi-track editing with 5+ tracks simultaneously (talking head, 

  B-roll, visuals, text overlays, audio, music)

- Voice-over-driven cutting: every cut must land precisely on the 

  spoken word. The voice-over sets the rhythm, NOT the music. This 

  is critical.

- Subtitle creation and styling: bold, colored, animated, with key 

  words highlighted in a different color. Subtitles must never cover 

  the face or important visuals.

- Text overlays for hooks, benefit callouts, and CTAs — cleanly 

  animated in and out, on-brand

- Infographic card overlays flown in over talking-head clips (e.g., a 

  card showing what each herb does, or a comparison table)

- Color correction and color grading to make AI-generated clips 

  visually consistent. AI clips often have slightly different color 

  temperature, saturation, and exposure — you must match them so the 

  final video looks like it came from one source.

- Audio mixing: voice-over loud and clear in the foreground, 

  background music subtle (15-20% of voice-over volume), clean 

  crossfades, no hard audio cuts

- Export in 9:16 (1080x1920) for Meta/TikTok, plus 1:1 versions when 

  requested



REQUIRED UNDERSTANDING

- Direct-response ad structure: Hook, Problem, Agitation, Mechanism, 

  Product Reveal, Social Proof, CTA. You don't need to write this — 

  but when editing you must FEEL where each section starts and ends 

  and match the visual rhythm to it.

- Pacing and cut rhythm: the hook (first 3 seconds) needs fast cuts 

  and visual surprise. The mechanism explanation needs slower cuts 

  so information lands. The CTA needs calm and clarity. You must 

  master this fast/slow alternation.

- Talking head ? B-roll switching every 3-8 seconds. Talking head 

  builds trust, B-roll proves or visualizes what is being said.

- Handling AI-generated clips: clips sometimes have artifacts, weird 

  first/last frames, imperfect lip-sync, unnatural hand movements. 

  You must know how to hide these issues — trim first/last frames, 

  use cutaways to B-roll at the right 
nt, light cropping/

  repositioning, smart transitions.

- Scroll-stopping hooks: the first 3 seconds d
 whether someone 

  keeps watching or scrolls away. You must be able to cut multiple 

  hook variations of the same ad for A/B testing.

- Audience awareness: our target is women 35-55, NOT Gen-Z TikTok 

  users. The aesthetic is warm, trustworthy, mature — NOT loud, 

  flashy, or Gen-Z. This is critical and most editors get it wrong.



NICE TO HAVE

- After Effects or DaVinci Resolve experience for animated 

  infographics, particle effects, kinetic typography

- Experience with Meta Ads Creative — knowledge of Hook Rate, Hold 

  Rate, Thumb-Stop Ratio

- Split-testing experience (different hooks, CTAs, visual styles)

- Experience editing health, wellness, or supplement-style ads with 

  a professional but not clinical look



WHAT YOU WILL RECEIVE FROM US

- Numbered clips (Clip 1 to Clip 46 etc.)

- Full ad copy as a text document

- Timing per clip with clear labels (talking head vs visual/B-roll)

- Final voice-over as audio file

- Mood references / example videos

- Brand colors, fonts, and logo files



WHAT YOU DELIVER

The fully edited video with subtitles, text overlays, music, and 

infographic overlays — export-ready in 9:16 (and 1:1 when requested).



WORK VOLUME & EXPECTATIONS

- Multiple new ads per week

- Fast turnarounds — first rough cut within 24-48 hours

- Heavy iteration — you will often be asked to cut 3 different hooks 

  for the same ad, change a CTA, or swap a scene. This is NOT 

  criticism, this is performance marketing. Editors who take 

  iteration personally will not work here.

- Creative freedom within structure: copy and clip order are fixed, 

  but transitions, zoom levels, text animations, and music choice 

  are yours.



GROWTH PATH

- Weeks 1-2: Paid trial period (1-2 ads to evaluate fit)

- Weeks 3-8: Part-time collaboration, building rhythm together

- Month 2+: If output and quality are strong, upgrade to full-time 

  position with higher monthly pay and lead editor responsibilities



LANGUAGE NOTE

The ads are in German, but you do NOT need to speak German. Subtitles 

will be reviewed by a German native speaker on our team. You only 

need to copy-paste subtitles correctly from the script we provide.



HOW TO APPLY

Please send:

1. Portfolio link (Google Drive, Vimeo, or website) — ideally with 

   at least 2 examples of direct-response or UGC-style ads you have 

   edited

2. Short answer (3-5 sentences) on this question: "When you receive 

   an AI-generated talking head clip with a weird first frame and 

   slightly off lip-sync, how do you handle it?"

3. Your CapCut Pro experience (months/years) and whether you also 

   use After Effects or DaVinci

4. Your available hours per week and time zone

5. Your rate (per hour OR per finished ad — your choice)



Applications without a portfolio will not be reviewed.

title: High-Ticket Sales Closer (Inbound Credit Repair & Funding Leads)
description:
We are looking for a confident and results-driven Sales Closer to convert warm, pre-qualified leads into paying clients.



All leads are inbound and booked calls—your role is to take control of the conversation, build trust, and close deals.



We specialize in credit repair and business funding solutions, helping clients improve their financial position and access capital.



If you’re someone who knows how to guide conversations, handle objections, and confidently ask for the sale—this role is for you.



?



Responsibilities:



* Conduct scheduled Zoom/phone calls with inbound leads

* Build rapport and understand client financial situations

* Clearly present our services and value

* Overcome objections with confidence and professionalism

* Close deals and collect payments

* Update CRM with call outcomes and notes



?



Requirements:



* Proven experience in sales or closing (high-ticket preferred)

* Strong English communication (clear, confident, minimal accent preferred)

* Ability to control conversations and lead clients to decisions

* Comfortable discussing money and asking for payment

* Reliable internet and quiet workspace

* Coachable and performance-driven mindset



?



Compensation:



* Base pay + commission OR commission-heavy (depending on experience)

* High earning potential based on performance

* Bonuses for hitting weekly/monthly targets



?



Hours:



* Flexible, but must be available for scheduled calls (U.S. time zones preferred)



?



To Apply:

Send:



1. A short voice note via WhatsApp (message for number) introducing yourself

2. Your sales/closing experience

3. Any results or deals you’ve closed
